description: AI Freight Agent MVP - Search and score loads from mock DAT data based on carrier truck parameters

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and database setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the FastAPI project structure and PostgreSQL database connection so all other stories have a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: FastAPI project with folder structure: app/, app/models/, app/routes/, app/services/, app/db/
PostgreSQL connection via SQLAlchemy async with database.py in app/db/
Alembic configured for migrations with alembic.ini and migrations/ folder
GET /health endpoint returns {"status": "ok"}
requirements.txt includes fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy[asyncio], asyncpg, alembic, pydantic
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation story — everything depends on this. Use Python 3.11+, async SQLAlchemy.

userStories.title: Carrier profile model and CRUD API
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want to store my company info (MC#, DOT#, insurance, contact) so the system knows who I am.
acceptanceCriteria: CarrierProfile model with fields: id, company_name, mc_number, dot_number, insurance_provider, insurance_policy_number, insurance_expiry (date), contact_name, contact_email, contact_phone, created_at, updated_at
Alembic migration creates carrier_profiles table
POST /api/carriers creates a carrier profile and returns it
GET /api/carriers/{id} retrieves a carrier profile
PUT /api/carriers/{id} updates a carrier profile
Validation: mc_number must be 6 digits, dot_number must be 7-8 digits
Returns 404 for non-existent carrier IDs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 for project structure and DB connection.

userStories.title: Truck model and CRUD API
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want to define my truck capabilities and preferences so the system filters loads I can actually haul.
acceptanceCriteria: Truck model with fields: id, carrier_id (FK to carrier_profiles), name, equipment_type (enum: dry_van, reefer, flatbed, step_deck, power_only), max_weight_lbs (int), current_city, current_state, current_lat (float), current_lng (float), max_deadhead_miles (int), min_rate_per_mile (decimal), created_at, updated_at
Alembic migration creates trucks table with foreign key to carrier_profiles
POST /api/trucks creates a truck (requires valid carrier_id)
GET /api/trucks/{id} retrieves a truck
PUT /api/trucks/{id} updates a truck
GET /api/trucks?carrier_id={id} lists all trucks for a carrier
Returns 400 if carrier_id does not exist
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 for carrier_profiles table.

userStories.title: Preferred lanes model and API
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want to save preferred lanes so the system prioritizes loads on routes I like to run.
acceptanceCriteria: PreferredLane model with fields: id, truck_id (FK to trucks), origin_city, origin_state, destination_city, destination_state, priority_weight (int 1-10 default 5), created_at
Alembic migration creates preferred_lanes table with FK to trucks
POST /api/trucks/{truck_id}/lanes adds a preferred lane
GET /api/trucks/{truck_id}/lanes lists lanes for a truck
DELETE /api/trucks/{truck_id}/lanes/{lane_id} removes a lane
Returns 404 if truck_id does not exist
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 for trucks table.

userStories.title: Mock DAT load data service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a mock data layer returning realistic loads so I can test search and scoring without a real API.
acceptanceCriteria: MockDATService class in app/services/mock_dat.py

Generates 50+ mock loads with fields: load_id, origin_city, origin_state, origin_lat, origin_lng, destination_city, destination_state, dest_lat, dest_lng, equipment_type, weight_lbs, rate_total, rate_per_mile, miles, pickup_date, delivery_date, broker_name, broker_mc
Loads cover all 5 equipment types with Southeast US focus
Rate range is $1.50-$4.00 per mile
search_loads(equipment_type, origin_lat, origin_lng, radius_miles) method filters by equipment type and uses Haversine formula to filter by distance from origin
Haversine distance calculation in a utility function app/services/geo.py
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No DB dependency. Uses same interface a real DAT adapter would so it can be swapped later.

userStories.title: Load search and filter endpoint
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want to search for loads matching my truck so I only see what I can haul near me.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/loads/search?truck_id={id} endpoint
Reads truck equipment_type, current_lat, current_lng, max_deadhead_miles, max_weight_lbs from DB
Calls MockDATService.search_loads() with truck parameters
Filters out loads where weight_lbs exceeds truck max_weight_lbs
Each result includes all load fields plus deadhead_miles (distance from truck to pickup)
Returns 404 if truck_id does not exist
Returns empty list with 200 if no loads match
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (truck data) and US-005 (mock loads).

userStories.title: Load scoring and ranking service
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want loads ranked by a score so the best ones for me appear first.
acceptanceCriteria: LoadScoringService class in app/services/scoring.py

Score formula: rate_per_mile_score (0-40 pts, higher rate = more pts), deadhead_score (0-30 pts, lower deadhead = more pts), lane_match_score (0-30 pts, bonus if origin+dest match a preferred lane, scaled by priority_weight)
Total score is 0-100
GET /api/loads/search?truck_id={id} now returns results sorted by score descending
Each result includes score (int) and score_breakdown object with rate_points, deadhead_points, lane_points
Loads below truck min_rate_per_mile are excluded from results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (preferred lanes) and US-006 (search endpoint).

userStories.title: Web dashboard - carrier and truck setup page
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want a web page to enter my company info and truck details instead of using raw API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: HTML page served at GET / with Carrier Profile form and Truck Setup form
Carrier form: company_name, mc_number, dot_number, contact_name, contact_email, contact_phone
Truck form: name, equipment_type (dropdown with 5 options), max_weight_lbs, current_city, current_state, max_deadhead_miles, min_rate_per_mile
Forms submit via JavaScript fetch() to POST /api/carriers and POST /api/trucks
Success and error messages display on page without reload
Clean simple CSS styling — no framework needed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 and US-003 APIs being working. Static HTML served by FastAPI.

userStories.title: Web dashboard - load search results page
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want to see scored load results in a table so I can quickly find the best loads.
acceptanceCriteria: HTML page served at GET /dashboard
Dropdown to select a truck (populated from GET /api/trucks?carrier_id=1)
Search Loads button calls GET /api/loads/search?truck_id={id}
Results table columns: Score, Origin, Destination, Miles, Rate/Mile, Total Rate, Deadhead, Equipment, Pickup Date, Broker
Table sorted by score descending
Score column color coded: green (70+), yellow (40-69), red (below 40)
Shows empty state message when no loads match
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 (scoring) and US-008 (setup page creates test data).

userStories.title: Web dashboard - preferred lanes management
userStories.description: As a carrier, I want to add and remove preferred lanes from the dashboard to influence load scoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Section on /dashboard page below the search results for managing preferred lanes
Add lane form: origin_city, origin_state, destination_city, destination_state, priority_weight (range slider 1-10)
Displays list of existing lanes for selected truck with delete button on each
Adding or removing a lane works via fetch() to the lanes API without page reload
After adding/removing a lane, re-searching shows updated scores
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (lanes API) and US-009 (dashboard page exists).
